## Highlights

- **Crash fix — tapping Connect with no Wi-Fi** — v1.0.70 / v1.0.71 SIGABRT-crashed when the user tapped "Connect TCP" while the device had no reachable network. Cause: another instance of the Kotlin/Native `@Throws` bridge gap (same shape as the v1.0.69 import-identity crash). `TcpSocket.connect`'s `error("getaddrinfo failed")` raised an `IllegalStateException` that bubbled up through `TcpInterface.connect` into Swift's `try await transport.connect()` — and without `@Throws` on the suspend function, K/N called `terminateWithUnhandledException` → `abort()` instead of delivering NSError to the Swift `do/catch`. Now the failure surfaces in the existing "Connection failed: …" red text below the Connect button, exactly like every other network error.
- **Transport sweep** — broadened the v1.0.69 engine-only `@Throws` sweep to cover the transport surface Swift touches directly: `TcpInterface.connect` / `disconnect`, `IosBleTransport.connect` / `disconnect` / `applyRadioConfig`, plus a couple of engine stragglers (`myIdentityCard`, `ourDestHash`) that the earlier sweep missed. The grep guard in the project memory is now `try await ` (broad) instead of `try await engine.*` (engine-only).

Companion tag: `android-v1.2.10` is a maintenance bump for lockstep version tracking — the change is no-op on Android (JVM ignores `@Throws` for unchecked exceptions); no user-visible behavior change.
